language: Albanian (Kosovo Gheg primary, standard for recognition)
source: CEFR A1 descriptors (functions layer per Core Inventory); standard Albanian reference grammar; Gheg forms verified against family usage by native-speaker teacher

cards.title: Greetings & courtesy
cards.goal: Can greet, respond to greetings, and use basic courtesy phrases naturally.
cards.evidence: Three greeting exchanges (morning, arriving, leaving) at natural speed, responding appropriately in all three.

cards.title: Introducing yourself
cards.goal: Can introduce him/herself and give basic personal information.
cards.evidence: 30-second self-introduction (name, origin, residence, one extra fact), then answer two follow-up questions.
skin_examples: a new relative at a gathering

cards.title: Family & having
cards.goal: Can name family members and say what he/she has.
lexis: Kosovo kinship terms as the family uses them: nana, baba, motra, vëllau, axha vs daja, halla vs tezja
cards.evidence: Walk through a real family photo naming six people with correct kinship terms and one kam-sentence each.

cards.title: One thing or THE thing (definiteness & gender)
cards.goal: Can use indefinite and definite noun forms and identify noun gender.
cards.evidence: For 10 familiar nouns, produce both forms and gender correctly for 8+, then use four in true sentences.
cards.note: The structural novelty from French: article as suffix. Gets its own paradigm table.

cards.title: Describing simply (linking article)
cards.goal: Can describe people and things with basic adjectives, correctly linked.
grammar: adjective + linking article agreement: djalë i mirë / vajzë e mirë
core articled adjectives: i madh/e madhe, i vogël/e vogël, i ri/e re
cards.evidence: Describe four people/things (two m., two f.) with two adjectives each; linking article correct in 6/8.

cards.title: What I do (present tense)
cards.goal: Can describe daily life and routines in the present.
grammar: high-frequency irregulars: ha, pi, flas, shkoj, vij, bëj/baj
lexis: time expressions: çdo ditë, në mëngjes, zakonisht
cards.evidence: Describe a typical day in six sentences including two about another person (3rd person).

cards.title: Right now (po + present)
cards.goal: Can say and ask what is happening at the moment.
cards.evidence: Answer Çka po ban? truthfully five times across a week of practice moments, and ask it back.

cards.title: Wants, likes & needs
cards.goal: Can say what he/she wants, likes and needs.
grammar: du me + participle (Gheg infinitive); standard dua të + conjugated for recognition
cards.evidence: Eight true sentences: three wants (2+ with du me + verb), three likes with më pëlqen, two needs; then answer A do…? questions.

cards.title: Can & may (mundem)
cards.goal: Can express ability and ask permission simply.
cards.evidence: Six true can/can't sentences plus two permission questions handled in a mini-roleplay.
skin_examples: what she can cook, say, understand so far

cards.title: Yes/no questions & saying no
cards.goal: Can ask yes/no questions with the a-particle and negate correctly.
grammar: a + verb questions: A je mirë? A do kafe?
negation nuk/s': s'du, nuk e di
cards.evidence: Question ping-pong: six a-questions asked, six truthful answers given, half negative, particle never dropped.
skin_examples: offering and accepting things around the house

cards.title: Numbers, prices & quantities
cards.goal: Can understand and use numbers, quantities and prices.
cards.evidence: Understand five spoken prices/numbers at natural speed; produce five (phone numbers, ages, prices).

cards.title: Time & days
cards.goal: Can tell the time and talk about days and parts of the day.
grammar: në orën tetë, në mëngjes/mbrëmje, sot/nesër/dje
cards.evidence: Answer five what-time questions and state three true schedule facts with correct time expressions.

cards.title: At the table (the hospitality script)
cards.goal: Can navigate Kosovo hospitality: accept, decline politely, ask for and offer things at the table.
lexis: core food and drink as the family cooks it
exponents: A han diçka? A pin kafe?
cards.evidence: Full table roleplay: offered five things — accept two, decline three politely, ask for one thing, compliment the food.
skin_examples: none needed — this is the real world
cards.note: Highest-value A1 card for her actual life.

cards.title: First past (kam + participle)
cards.goal: Can make simple statements about past events.
grammar: perfect: kam + participle — the everyday spoken past in Kosovo
Gheg short participles for production: kam punu, kam shku, e kam pa; standard long forms for recognition
kam qenë / jam kanë per family usage
cards.evidence: Five true sentences about yesterday/last weekend using kam + participle, forms per family norm.

cards.title: Connecting ideas
cards.goal: Can link simple sentences with basic connectors.
cards.evidence: Six connected sentences (spoken or short WhatsApp message) with at least two of each connector, used correctly.
skin_examples: a voice note about her day
cards.note: Mirrors the CEFR A1 coherence criterion.
